title: Skilled metal, electrical and electronic trades supervisors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. Manage or coordinate fitness and wellness programs and services. Manage and train staff of wellness specialists, health educators, or fitness instructors. (Based on 15 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Suggest or initiate personnel actions, such as promotions, transfers, or hires; recommend or execute personnel actions, such as hirings, evaluations, or promotions.

Record information, such as personnel, production, or operational data on specified forms or reports; maintain operations data, such as time, production, and cost records, and prepare management reports of production results; read and analyze charts, work orders, production schedules, and other records and reports to determine production requirements and evaluate current production estimates and outputs.

Provide assistance to workers engaged in construction or extraction activities, using hand tools or other equipment; train workers in construction methods, operation of equipment, safety procedures, or company policies; train staff in the use or sale of products, programs, or activities; conduct employee training in equipment operations or work and safety procedures, or assign employee training to experienced workers.

Analyze worker or production problems and recommend solutions, such as improving production methods or implementing motivational plans; recommend or implement measures to motivate employees and to improve production methods, equipment performance, product quality, or efficiency.

Arrange for repairs of equipment or machinery; order or requisition materials or supplies; estimate materials, equipment, and personnel needed for residential or commercial solar installation projects.

Visit customer sites to determine solar system needs, requirements, or specifications; provide technical assistance to installers, technicians, or other solar professionals in areas such as solar electric systems, solar thermal systems, electrical systems, or mechanical systems; inspect work progress, equipment, or construction sites to verify safety or to ensure that specifications are met.

Supervise solar installers, technicians, and subcontractors for solar installation projects to ensure compliance with safety standards; supervise, coordinate, or schedule the activities of construction or extractive workers; monitor work of contractors and subcontractors to ensure projects conform to plans, specifications, schedules, or budgets.

Identify means to reduce costs, minimize risks, or increase efficiency of solar installation projects; assess potential solar installation sites to determine feasibility and design requirements; assess system performance or functionality at the system, subsystem, and component levels.

Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in the production or processing of goods, such as inspectors, machine setters, or fabricators; assign work to employees, based on material or worker requirements of specific jobs; plan and establish work schedules, assignments, and production sequences to meet production goals; calculate labor and equipment requirements and production specifications, using standard formulas.

Estimate material or worker requirements to complete jobs; confer with management or subordinates to resolve worker problems, complaints, or grievances; evaluate employee performance and suggest ways to improve work; monitor operations to ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards; check spa equipment to ensure proper functioning.

Plan and coordinate installations of photovoltaic (PV) solar and solar thermal systems to ensure conformance to codes; design or implement improvements in communication, monitoring, or enforcement of compliance standards; develop or implement plans for the sustainable regeneration of brownfield sites to ensure regeneration of a wider area by providing environmental protection or economic and social benefits; coordinate facility schedules to maximize usage and efficiency.

Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions; interpret specifications, blueprints, job orders, and company policies and procedures for workers; confer with other supervisors to coordinate operations and activities within or between departments; keep records of employees' attendance and hours worked; evaluate regulatory affairs aspects that are specifically green, such as the use of toxic substances in packaging, carbon footprinting issues, or green policy implementation.

Communicate regulatory information to multiple departments and ensure that information is interpreted correctly; develop or implement marketing strategies; discuss emerging compliance issues to ensure that management and employees are informed about compliance reporting systems, policies, and practices; monitor emerging trends regarding industry regulations to determine potential impacts on organizational processes.

Conduct quantitative risk assessments for human health, environmental, or other risks; conduct feasibility or cost-benefit studies for environmental remediation projects; evaluate new software publishing systems and confer with regulatory agencies concerning news or updates on electronic publishing of submissions.

Direct environmental programs, such as air or water compliance, hazardous waste or materials management, solid waste recycling; monitor regulatory affairs activities to ensure their alignment with corporate sustainability or green initiatives; evaluate subcontractors or subcontractor bids for quality, cost, and reliability.

Perform cash audits and deposit investigations to fully account for store cash; report violations of compliance or regulatory standards to duly authorized enforcement agencies as appropriate or required; manage activities such as audits, regulatory agency inspections, or product recalls; prepare management reports regarding compliance operations and progress.

Resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered; respond to customer inquiries or complaints; interview and hire associates to fill staff vacancies; investigate product complaints and prepare documentation and submissions to appropriate regulatory agencies as necessary; store and retrieve equipment, such as vehicles, radios, and ride components.

Plan or direct spa services and programs; identify and apply for project funding; organize and oversee fitness or wellness programs, such as information presentations, blood drives, or training in first aid or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR); explain rules and regulations of facilities and entertainment attractions to customers; monitor compliance systems to ensure their effectiveness.

Clean equipment and areas of amusement park, cruise ship, or other recreational facility; operate, drive, or explain the use of mechanical equipment in amusement parks or cruise ships; schedule staff or supervise scheduling; train staff in regulatory policies or procedures; participate in continuing education classes to maintain current knowledge of the industry; keep informed regarding pending industry changes, trends, or best practices.
Conduct internal reviews or audits to ensure compliance procedures are followed, including investigations of compliance issues and maintaining documentation of compliance activities.
Provide recommendations and solutions in crisis situations, advise on loss-investigation procedures, and develop strategies to eliminate potential losses.
Visit stores and oversee areas such as surveillance, security systems, and compliance with company policies and procedures to enhance loss prevention.
Train loss prevention staff and retail managers on loss control measures, safety regulations, and compliance-related topics.
Supervise the implementation and effectiveness of wellness and fitness programs, evaluate needs assessments or surveys, and maintain fitness-related records.
Manage or coordinate fitness activities, develop wellness classes, and respond to inquiries regarding wellness programs.
Prepare and maintain budgets and operational plans for wellness services, fitness programs, and environmental projects, including project management activities.
Oversee environmental assessments and coordinate projects related to wind energy and environmental remediation, including contractor supervision.
Analyze data related to theft or fraud trends and collaborate with law enforcement to resolve issues.
Assess security needs and advise on compliance with applicable codes and regulations for retail and environmental initiatives.
Organize events and marketing campaigns to promote wellness, fitness participation, and community health initiatives.
Maintain physical equipment and facilities related to fitness and wellness, ensuring proper operation and safety standards are met while developing operational budgets.
Negotiate contracts for environmental and wind project services, and review relevant proposals or bids for project requirements.
Prepare documentation and reports regarding project status, environmental assessments, and compliance metrics for management and stakeholders.
Coordinate training sessions for fitness and wellness staff and ensure compliance with safety and operational procedures.
Develop partnerships with community resources to enhance wellness programs and improve safety measures at health-related events.
Lead audits of loss prevention programs, ensuring adherence to safety and compliance standards as well as providing guidance on necessary improvements.
Supervise wellness specialists and collaborate with human resources to develop consistent disciplinary action strategies regarding policy violations.
